title: C++ not Python — CS 161 summer-prep + Chiquito's graduation
html:
TL;DR

  • May 2022 admin note clarifying that OSU's cs 161 uses C++, not python — and that Yahir should join the CS Discord for summer learning.
  • Summer-class registration plan: MTH 251, "other," and CS 161 — a self-paced sophomore-summer push to keep on track.
  • Mentions Chiquito's graduation (decision pending) and a reminder to "let kaisa know" — same Kaisa from 2022 04 28 osig workbook (study-buddy / decision-loop peer).
  • Project 2 finish + MTH 231 extra-credit assignment — small datapoint on the parallel-grind shape of 2022 coursework.
  • Half-line "apply for more jobs , maybe not" — captures the sophomore-summer ambivalence about working-vs-studying that resurfaces in 2022 05 27 dinky summer gig.

Why it matters

Confirms that Yahir's first serious programming language at OSU was C++ (via cs 161), not python — important context for why his later self-taught Python and JavaScript work sometimes reads as outsider-coming-in. The github copilot + Python push doesn't appear until late 2022 (2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream).

The casual "apply for more jobs , maybe not" coupled with the next-week "do our own dinky gig" note shows the gig-vs-job toggle Yahir was running before the Brotein / founder framing crystallized.

Open questions

  • Who is "Chiquito"? (Likely a family-network peer / cousin — graduation coordination.)
  • Did he actually take CS 161 that summer, or push it to fall?
  • Was the CS Discord he joined an OSU-internal one or general-purpose?
